row089_contract.title: Surface, object, clothing, and contact-material recognition
row089_contract.completion_criteria: Material decisions fuse independent evidence and abstain or broaden class when visual proof is ambiguous.

architecture.authority_boundary: Fuse scene registry, tracks, masks, landmarks, flow, depth, material, and contact evidence; preserve observed, inferred, inherited, and unknown distinctions.

qa_protocol.authority_boundary: Filename and path tags, embeddings, acoustic features, tags or captions, script expectations, and reference comparisons are independent signals; no single signal grants PASS and disagreement lowers confidence or requires fallback or abstention.

taxonomy.boundary: wood_floor and cotton_fabric are plausible broader aliases, but no governed alias map exists and skin is absent. The taxonomy does not classify pixels, regions, objects, clothing, or contacts.

foley_profiles.boundary: These are downstream source and force profiles, not visual recognition evidence.

implementation.method: first filename substring match else unspecified

filename_material_heuristic_partial_auth.boundary: This deterministic audio-index metadata heuristic is not an image or region material classifier. It has no segmentation, texture, contact, provenance fusion, confidence calibration, or governed abstention authority.

material_surface_profile.boundary: The schema cannot bind a classifier observation, region or track, provenance sources, calibrated confidence, class hierarchy, ambiguity, or abstention.

foley_force_material_event.boundary: source_material_id and target_material_id consume nonempty identifiers but do not prove how a visual material decision was produced.

audio_asset_intelligence_record.boundary: The semantics block supports an arbitrary material string, confidence, and evidence sources for downstream audio assets; it is not visual material recognition authority.

skin_material_qa_partial_authority.boundary: This stack evaluates rendering and continuity against a declared skin-material goal. It does not detect or classify skin from arbitrary visual regions, and its own promotion authority remains blocked by missing trusted regional mask evidence.

clothing_contact_qa_partial_authority.boundary: This stack governs declared clothing, prop, and support-contact realism. It does not infer material class from pixels or fuse material evidence into a calibrated recognition decision.

segmentation_refinement.boundary: A bounded SAM2 face refinement exists, but consensus and canonical polygon authority are absent and it is not a material-classification benchmark.

focused_test_result.scope: Adjacent audio-index, skin-material-contract, and clothing-contact-contract behavior only

required_visual_material_recognition_con: A governed scene-registry observation bound to frame, shot, take, actor, object, clothing region, and contact region identifiers.
A production image or region material classifier with exact model, weights, preprocessing, class map, and implementation hashes.
Owner-aware segmentation or tracking inputs that bind each observation to the correct surface rather than a whole-frame guess.
Texture evidence with raw feature provenance, resolution and crop authority, and deterministic quality gates.
Contact context fused from actor-object-region ownership rather than declared labels alone.
Independent evidence-source provenance and a deterministic fusion rule that prevents one weak signal from granting PASS.
Calibrated confidence with class-specific thresholds and benchmarked error or confusion evidence.
An executable broader-class and abstention policy for ambiguity, disagreement, occlusion, low resolution, and unsupported classes.
A governed alias hierarchy covering hardwood or wood_floor, fabric or cotton_fabric, and skin without silent taxonomy substitution.
A strict output schema binding observed class, broader class, confidence, provenance, region, contact context, ambiguity, and abstention reason.
Positive, negative, ambiguity, false-positive, occlusion, reappearance, and cross-frame consistency tests.
A representative annotated visual benchmark for surfaces, objects, clothing, skin, and contact materials.
Runtime evidence and combined frame, contact, and audio review for the exact Row089 output.
Completed Row085 tracking authority and Row088 depth or source-position authority.

acceptance_matrix_required.acceptance: TRK-W64-085 and TRK-W64-088 each have row_complete=true with hash-bound evidence.
A runtime scene registry provides immutable frame or span and entity-region identity inputs.
A hash-bound visual classifier and class map produce deterministic region-level candidates.
Texture features and quality gates are recorded per classified region.
Contact ownership and participating surfaces are proven rather than inferred from filenames.
Calibrated confidence and class-specific thresholds are benchmarked and recorded.
Ambiguous or unsupported inputs deterministically broaden class or abstain with a reason.
Every decision binds independent source observations and the fusion decision.
Required classes and governed aliases are complete, unique, and schema-bound.
The exact tracker output, runtime record, test log, and combined frame-contact-audio QA pass are present.

checks.detail: Audit source commit is exact current HEAD 746f3d2c.

checks.detail: Tracker and item requirements JSON files are byte-identical.

checks.name: worker_and_runtime_containment

checks.name: row_complete
checks.detail: Implementation, tests, runtime, QA, and dependencies do not support completion.

scheduler_and_worker_containment.note: The audit does not re-enable or alter paused Codex automations, disabled AI-worker tasks, disabled Mask maintenance tasks, or the EC2 read-only safety monitor.

hold_decision.reason: The repository contains useful taxonomy, declared-material QA, contact QA, and bounded segmentation evidence, but these do not constitute production visual material recognition. Both dependencies remain incomplete, and direct scene-registry, classifier, texture, contact, confidence, provenance, abstention, benchmark, runtime, and combined-review authority are absent.
hold_decision.safe_next_work: Implement the missing contract only through a disjoint signed worker scope when eligible, beginning with strict output schema and benchmark fixtures while preserving dependency gates. Codex may continue independent tracker audits without waiting for worker-control qualification.
